campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's financial records from July 2025, including how the city's investments performed and overall money management. The council also discussed plans for improving transportation options across Campbell, looking at ways to make getting around town easier for residents using different types of transportation.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment performance and financial transactions
City treasurer's monthly financial report
New multimodal transportation plan to improve local transit options

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public meeting on March 18, 2026 where residents had a chance to speak about a proposed new rule or policy. After hearing from the public, the council introduced the ordinance and decided to skip the normal first reading process to speed things up, with plans to vote on final approval at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held - residents could comment on proposed ordinance
First reading waived to expedite approval process
Ordinance scheduled for final vote at next regular council meeting
Consent calendar placement approved for adoption

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve records from several previous meetings held in March and to move forward with a construction contract. The council reviewed and formally approved minutes from multiple special meetings and study sessions, and authorized the Town Manager to sign a construction agreement with QLM, Inc.
keyTopics: Approved March meeting records and minutes
Authorized construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Reviewed closed session discussions from March meetings

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 14, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to handle several administrative and infrastructure matters. The council dealt with staff absences, filled positions on city boards and commissions, and approved contract adjustments for two major water projects managed by engineering firms.
keyTopics: Contract changes for wastewater treatment facility upgrades
Budget increase for South Bay Water Recycling Pump Station 5 project
New appointments to city boards and commissions
Work with engineering firms HDR and CDM Smith on construction oversight

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to discuss the city's spending plans for the next fiscal year, including which infrastructure and improvement projects will get funding. The council also officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and volunteers in the community.
keyTopics: 2026-27 budget for city construction and infrastructure projects
Teen Month proclamation
National Volunteer Week recognition
Capital improvement program planning

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 14, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review previous meeting notes and introduced a new proposed rule change about tenant protections for renters in Sunnyvale. The meeting covered updates to the city's residential tenant protection policies, which affect how landlords and renters are regulated.
keyTopics: Approval of March 25, 2026 meeting minutes
New ordinance to amend tenant protection rules (Chapter 19.71)
Residential tenant protections program updates
Public hearing on housing and renter policies

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 13, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council held a workshop to help residents understand and use OpenGov, a tool that shows how Cupertino spends its money. The meeting also covered rules about who has to register if they're trying to influence city decisions, like lobbyists.
keyTopics: OpenGov financial transparency tool demo and community training
City budget spending and how residents can track it
New lobbying registration and reporting requirements
Rules for people who try to influence city council decisions

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 14, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on April 14, 2026 using both in-person and online options so residents could participate however they preferred. The city is making it easy for people to attend and share their thoughts by offering Zoom access and phone dial-in options.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format available (in-person and Zoom)
Remote participation via Zoom (Meeting ID: 997-0675-9306)
Phone access option at 1(669) 900-6833
Written public comments accepted through eComment system on City Council Agenda page

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on April 7, 2026 to handle several administrative matters and planning items. The main focus was reviewing a major update to the city's 5-year housing and community development plan, considering a change to how council vacancies are filled, and approving meeting minutes and calendars.
keyTopics: Update to city's 2025-2030 housing and development plan
Change to Council Vacancy Ordinance rules
Individual councilmembers' new workplan requests
Approval of previous meeting minutes
Preview of upcoming agenda items

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council held a public hearing about a historic building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown. The building's owner is asking to upgrade its historic status, which would allow them to add 5,000 extra square feet of floor space in exchange for fixing up the building and making it safer against earthquakes.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street historic building upgrade request
5,000 square feet of bonus floor space for seismic and historic rehabilitation
Elevation from Category 4 to Category 2 historic resource status
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule and assignments
Downtown Commercial zone development
